shortAnswer: MCHC measures the concentration of hemoglobin in red blood cells. Low values may indicate iron deficiency anemia or certain other types of anemia. Highly elevated values are rare. Discuss any abnormal results with your doctor.
metaDescription: MCHC low or high? Normal ranges, meaning, and causes of abnormal hemoglobin concentration — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: What type of anemia could explain my MCHC?
pageTitle: MCHC value — Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentration explained | Medyra
